headline: 观看：预计将出现G1级地磁风暴
impacts_text: 影响区域主要位于地磁纬度60度以北。感应电流 - 可能出现微弱的电网波动。航天器 - 可能对卫星运行产生轻微影响。极光 - 在高纬度地区（如美国北部，例如密歇根州北部和缅因州）可能可见极光。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: WATA20
Serial Number: 1109
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 2350 UTC

WATCH: Geomagnetic Storm Category G1 Predicted

Highest Storm Level Predicted by Day:
Apr 25:  G1 (Minor)   Apr 26:  G1 (Minor)   Apr 27:  None (Below G1)

THIS SUPERSEDES ANY/ALL PRIOR WATCHES IN EFFECT

NOAA Space Weather Scale descriptions can be found at
www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

Potential Impacts: Area of impact primarily poleward of 60 degrees Geomagnetic Latitude.
Induced Currents - Weak power grid fluctuations can occur.
Spacecraft - Minor impact on satellite operations possible.
Aurora - Aurora may be visible at high latitudes, i.e., northern tier of the U.S. such as northern Michigan and Maine.

details.apr_25: G1 (Minor)   Apr 26:  G1 (Minor)   Apr 27:  None (Below G1)

headline: 摘要：X射线事件超过M5级
impacts_text: 影响区域主要集中在地球向阳面的太阳直射点。无线电 - 高频（HF）无线电通信出现持续数十分钟的有限中断。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: SUMXM5
Serial Number: 313
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 1835 UTC

SUMMARY: X-ray Event exceeded M5
Begin Time: 2026 Apr 24 1754 UTC
Maximum Time: 2026 Apr 24 1815 UTC
End Time: 2026 Apr 24 1830 UTC
X-ray Class: M6.4
Location: N19W90
NOAA Scale: R2 - Moderate

NOAA Space Weather Scale descriptions can be found at
www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

Potential Impacts: Area of impact centered primarily on sub-solar point on the sunlit side of Earth.
Radio - Limited blackout of HF (high frequency) radio communication for tens of minutes.

headline: 警报：X射线通量超过M5级
impacts_text: 影响区域集中在地球向阳面的太阳直射点。高频（HF）无线电通信中断的范围取决于当前的X射线通量强度。有关受影响区域和预期持续时间的实时信息，请访问 http://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/d-region-absorption-predictions-d-rap。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: ALTXMF
Serial Number: 525
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 1812 UTC

ALERT: X-Ray Flux exceeded M5
Threshold Reached: 2026 Apr 24 1812 UTC
NOAA Scale: R2 - Moderate

NOAA Space Weather Scale descriptions can be found at
www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

Potential Impacts: Area of impact centered on sub-solar point on the sunlit side of Earth. Extent of blackout of HF (high frequency) radio communication dependent upon current X-ray Flux intensity. For real-time information on affected area and expected duration please see http://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/d-region-absorption-predictions-d-rap.

headline: 警报：IV型射电辐射
description_text: IV型辐射与太阳上的重大爆发有关，通常与强烈的日冕物质抛射和太阳辐射风暴相关联。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: ALTTP4
Serial Number: 707
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 1041 UTC

ALERT: Type IV Radio Emission
Begin Time: 2026 Apr 24 0840 UTC



Description: Type IV emissions occur in association with major eruptions on the sun and are typically associated with strong coronal mass ejections and solar radiation storms.

headline: 警报：II型射电辐射
description_text: II型辐射与太阳上的爆发有关，通常表明日冕物质抛射与耀斑事件相关。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: ALTTP2
Serial Number: 1479
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 0928 UTC

ALERT: Type II Radio Emission
Begin Time: 2026 Apr 24 0815 UTC
Estimated Velocity: 1293 km/s

Comment: Reissued with adjusted shock speed.



Description: Type II emissions occur in association with eruptions on the sun and typically indicate a coronal mass ejection is associated with a flare event.

headline: 摘要：10厘米射电爆发
description_text: 10厘米射电爆发表明与太阳耀斑相关的10厘米波长电磁爆发强度达到或超过了初始10厘米射电背景的两倍。这可能预示着与太阳耀斑相关的显著射电噪声。这种噪声通常持续时间较短，但可能对包括雷达、GPS和卫星通信在内的敏感接收器造成干扰。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: SUM10R
Serial Number: 908
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 0838 UTC

SUMMARY: 10cm Radio Burst
Begin Time: 2026 Apr 24 0809 UTC
Maximum Time: 2026 Apr 24 0811 UTC
End Time: 2026 Apr 24 0815 UTC
Duration: 6 minutes
Peak Flux: 570 sfu
Latest Penticton Noon Flux: 128 sfu



Description: A 10cm radio burst indicates that the electromagnetic burst associated with a solar flare at the 10cm wavelength was double or greater than the initial 10cm radio background. This can be indicative of significant radio noise in association with a solar flare. This noise is generally short-lived but can cause interference for sensitive receivers including radar, GPS, and satellite communications.

headline: 摘要：X射线事件超过X1级
impacts_text: 影响区域包括地球向阳面的大部分地区，在太阳直射点处最强。无线电——高频（HF）无线电通信在大范围内中断约一小时。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: SUMX01
Serial Number: 216
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 0826 UTC

SUMMARY: X-ray Event exceeded X1
Begin Time: 2026 Apr 24 0801 UTC
Maximum Time: 2026 Apr 24 0813 UTC
End Time: 2026 Apr 24 0818 UTC
X-ray Class: X2.5
Optical Class: 2b
Location: N20W90
NOAA Scale: R3 - Strong

NOAA Space Weather Scale descriptions can be found at
www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

Potential Impacts: Area of impact consists of large portions of the sunlit side of Earth, strongest at the sub-solar point.
Radio - Wide area blackout of HF (high frequency) radio communication for about an hour.

headline: 警报：X射线通量超过M5级
impacts_text: 影响区域以地球向阳面的太阳直射点为中心。高频（HF）无线电通信的中断范围取决于当前的X射线通量强度。有关受影响区域和预期持续时间的实时信息，请访问 http://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/d-region-absorption-predictions-d-rap。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: ALTXMF
Serial Number: 524
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 24 0812 UTC

ALERT: X-Ray Flux exceeded M5
Threshold Reached: 2026 Apr 24 0812 UTC
NOAA Scale: R2 - Moderate

NOAA Space Weather Scale descriptions can be found at
www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

Potential Impacts: Area of impact centered on sub-solar point on the sunlit side of Earth. Extent of blackout of HF (high frequency) radio communication dependent upon current X-ray Flux intensity. For real-time information on affected area and expected duration please see http://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/d-region-absorption-predictions-d-rap.

Potential Impacts: Satellite systems may experience significant charging resulting in increased risk to satellite systems.

Space Weather Message Code: WARK04
Serial Number: 5324
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 21 0110 UTC

EXTENDED WARNING: Geomagnetic K-index of 4 expected
Extension to Serial Number: 5323
Valid From: 2026 Apr 20 0805 UTC
Now Valid Until: 2026 Apr 21 1200 UTC
Warning Condition: Persistence

NOAA Space Weather Scale descriptions can be found at
www.swpc.noaa.gov/noaa-scales-explanation

Potential Impacts: Area of impact primarily poleward of 65 degrees Geomagnetic Latitude.
Induced Currents - Weak power grid fluctuations can occur.
Aurora - Aurora may be visible at high latitudes such as Canada and Alaska.

headline: 持续警报：2MeV电子积分通量超过1000pfu
impacts_text: 卫星系统可能会经历显著充电，导致卫星系统风险增加。
message_raw:
Space Weather Message Code: ALTEF3
Serial Number: 3680
Issue Time: 2026 Apr 20 2023 UTC

CONTINUED ALERT: Electron 2MeV Integral Flux exceeded 1000pfu
Continuation of Serial Number: 3679
Begin Time: 2026 Apr 19 1920 UTC
Yesterday Maximum 2MeV Flux: 1297 pfu



Potential Impacts: Satellite systems may experience significant charging resulting in increased risk to satellite systems.
